text: june 22nd This morning when I woke up I thought about all the hoonies and how it’s just a game I made for myself and set theory is also a game I made for myself, and thought about re-imagining the book as a component of game theory. Well, game theory is the first step on the making up games. making up games for myself. Playing made up games. anyway. as I was lying in bed I was thinking - Set theory is also a game I made up for myself So: another possible outline (?): 1. game theory - make up games for ourself → Systems of Movement - when we get stuck, it’s nice to have a place to go And sometimes we can make up games for ourselves, using all the components we like the most

June 24th
all the hoonies
your mind should be in the center - try not to let it stray to the periphery, where the things are, instead concentrate on the empty space in the middle, and what all the things refer to. You may think to yourself - “no, this one isn’t quite right” or “this one doesn't belong here,”

beautiful placeholder The Imaginator (the imagination station) You use all these (cards) to imagine something. (I wanted to prop it up - hang it - so I could see it while I’m working - what I’m trying to imagine… OR: I guess, it can hold all the things you’re trying to remember all the ways you’re lying to yourself - all the things you’re trying to convince yourself - so you don’t have to…creating a physical form for your thoughts so that, when you ex. discard all of them - dismiss them “oh, those don’t count” you can leave some (and have them still be there) all the things I need to imagine something. bird + pic → it’s hard that they’re on the same card, and there’s 1 of them but they’re not together → the idea that some things don’t quite make sense, and you sue this to accept something that’s hard to accept - that something you can change shouldn’t be changed (serenity prayer tehe) - → something soft - an angel - I’m trying to be nice to myself. - → the candle (?) - I’m trying to foster excitement (these are all the things I need) → the clock + the eye: “where I am is a strange place” @@PB@@

26th Similitude - “something in common” (ニ()ニ) → this can be the basis exercise: do this with a friend, lover or stranger: what do the two of you have in common/ Draw a (large) venn diagram and use this to write out all the things you have in common and your differences. You may want to draw the internal ellipse in proportion to how much you have in common you can plug anything in - data - to get the “raw form” (even moments can be data) The drawer in your mind - the drawer pull - a Cognitive Vehicle is like a drawer pull in that it is the key to opening the drawer - without it, you have nothing to hold onto. (Don’t forget to write what the things have in common with their cognitive vehicles/metaphors in the ellipse

other way to star (or should it be like this (pic)? balloon metaphor at some point (ニ()ニ) ^ Similitude → this is what these have in common… Here’s the structure: () or, you could just take this part of it…so that’s ONE relationship that two things could have in common. (pic) another one is the game set. (pic (apparatus)) ← these cards all have some things in common, and some differences. Here is another relationship (pic) implies they have something in common with each other (implies the center) or they have 1 thing in common with the center. Another thing you can do is But anyway - now that you have the thing in the the middle you can apply it to other things…or you can start looking for examples of it (or maybe your brain will do that automatically)

MENTAL STEWING (pic)
(pic)
as the stew stays on the stove, things begin to get clumped together - things that are similar
till finally, you have clumps of things that have something in common.
This may be how your brain sorts through memories - it starts with disparate parts, and if you allow them to “stew,” you will begin to see connections between disparate one's and form little collections of certain types of events.
The Ambiguous event is (the cornerstone) - at the center of everything - like a quantum mechanics - it could be one or the other and you can’t determine which.

(pic)
(pic) ← membrane (this is the actual event that happened, and the story you’re telling yourself about it
← the possible interpretations that could be true about it if they were proven - true elsewhere…
once we understand the simplicity and beauty of the ambiguous event (AE), we can use it to do lots of things. retroactive pattern manipulation. proactive pattern manipulation.
(pic)
kits you could make → containers with the wrong thing inside of them. paradoxes - things that disagree with each other - pieces of things [metaphysically, these are blind spots in the brain, where - when we try to use logic for something
(pic)

Because the vast majority of tings live in this space, we forget about the other things, and think logic applies to everything. We can apply logic to everything, and the result will be illogical (ex, the ship of theseus) on the periphery, our ability to categorize goes buh bye and we end up with fun illogical nonsense like the ship of theseus and pieces of things - questions like “what is a thingamajig (a thingamajig is a placeholder and so non-specific, however, in english this still makes sense as a question. We also have things that look like paradoxes as they confuse us in a similar way O→←O “is it this, or is it this?” so it leads to the same place in our brain. “A Metaphor is a picture of a pattern” - it tells us what the pattern is about but it doesn’t actually “work” (the caboose). in a way, a game is a cognitive vehicle - it gets us to a certain place in our brain - to use our brain in a certain way - specifically, the goal prescribes the way we need to act in order to achieve it, thereby dictating a certain pattern of behavior. Working backwards, we can encourage/invite a certain pattern of behavior by devising a rule and a goal to work in tandem with each other (as a system), thereby activating the behavior we want (solve for…) a + b = c

June 27th
I think my new hobby is hating people. I’m looking at them, trying to think of things to hate about them. Ideas about garnets when I got home yesterday in time for work and realized I would make (at least pics) the components of the BARPK on AI - mixed feelings or I guess bitter but I know I have to do it. I do think it will become as intrinsic to every process as using a computer soon.

Need to make more hoonies (print em out) and cut the size of the smallest wooden box - Also could put XI in there (that many cards?) That Hoonies are easier to make a lot of cards for Then maybe it would be satisfying and useful to imagine things with them - so many and better one's. Also - easy, simple “Set” game - two random cards in slot - lay out 12, and choose the best one. OR just play it like SET and everyone looks and either 1. takes a set or 2. finds the best Set and compete. Discussion unless it’s obvious - could even state the characteristics they have in common - special papers (similar/diff). (missing) (pic)

June 29th - journal entry Now I have a ritual of writing in my diary. It’s confusing cause I told Marlon I wanted to, but not with that urgent need as most of my projets. And now I’m doing it everyday. I go out, thinking about my projects, bring my journal, go to the coffee shop, can’t work on anything, but think “Oh, well, I’ll write in my diary - I guess I did do some things last night.” And then I do, and it’s very fulfilling and feels good to write and I think when will I stop, but there’s a time that feels natural so then I do, and usually leave, intending to do something else. And then I say “Oh, went to the coffee shop and wrote in my journal” but that doesn’t seem like a possible ritual, because people write in their journal at night but that does not give me enough time to process everything - still raw and fresh in my mind. I guess the idea that there's no clear purpose makes it easier to perform as a ritual.

I was thinking on the hike, when I lost my balance - that I’m good at balancing, and thought about going off balance like by thinking about J too much nd kind of the way to go back on balance is to slowly come in in concentric circles (smaller) going a little the other way, etc. (and that I’m good at that. that affirmation helped a lot. i could tell Maddie that. Cause they are emotionally intelligent. I could tell mommy. I could maybe tell Sean - yesterday I told him that I descended into bad thoughts as soon as I got home - he left - which I knew I would which is why I didn't want him to leave - maybe why Sarah and Niha are desperate to keep hanging out. But when I work on SET theory - they’re not there and it’s better than hanging out with people.

The coffee cake I got - thought it would have coffee - mental face palm - that hike - (?) - I wish people would say “contact” when I was talking to them - cause too often I have no idea, if they are hearing me - “do you know what I mean?” is a desperate cry for help. (At this point I’m just pretending the coffee bread was chocolate chips - it’s kind of working - (and I am so clever, and I am so kind).

July 4th A plant is very much in my face and it’s making my life a living hell - my peripheral vision is fucked and I keep getting surprised to see it even though I know it’s there (pic) did a spit take cause like what is she wearing (pic) guy was wearing pants like this (pic) Homeless guy moved from next to me so that I can still charge my comp but not be eaten by the plant. Tried my spinach but it was disgusting - felt like popeye for a sec though. Banana makes sense. So many ugly people and old people today. Normally not like this. And I hate them for being fat and old and not cute and beautiful. I don’t like any of the people here right now. i think I would not like them if I got to know them in fact, I think my hatred would grow if I followed them to their home, if they invited me for tea at their house - I could look through the stained glass windows in their front room - I know there’s a house like that North of Montana and I think they all live there, or at least one.

Now pasty white bitches wearing grandma shorts are here - giant purses I don’t like them, but I don’t know if I’d say I hate them. Someone bought a free will is overrated hat. One of my faves. i love all the people walking by outside. I’m so excited for my (?). Went to bed early last night - working on my presentation which is going not badly - there’s more parts to it than anything else, and I can just do the outline for each part doesn’t take that long. Also: there’s so many ways to write the text, but maybe it’s better to just speak it. (note: the rest of the journal is filled with drawings for my powerpoint that I was working on at the time. I guess maybe I just work because it feels good to use my mind but if I finish something, then it involves the kinds of work i don’t like? idk (maybe))
